free_text: Excitation functions of proton-induced reactions on
natural Nd in the 10-30 MeV energy range, and
production of radionuclides relevant for double-beta
decay
U-120M isochronous cyclotron
17 natural Nd targets (10x10 mm2) prepared from
commercially available Nd foils (99.9% purity).
The foil thickness was measured using low-energy gamma
ray (241Am source) absorption and was equal to
Each sample was coated with very thin parylene-C layer
for protection against oxidization (2-3 micro-m).
The beam energy was calculated from the precisely
measured beam orbit position and its decrease in the
various materials was calculated using the code SRIM.
55% NaI(Tl) efficiency at 1332.5 keV.
The spectrometer was calibrated by a set of standards.
The measured efficiencies were fitted with analytical
functions.
Activities were measured at different sample-detector
distances.
The detector dead time was <40%, preferably <20%.
Update of IAEA-TECDOC-1211, Vienna (2001). Available
online at http://nds.iaea.org/medical/.
Estimated uncertainties of the beam energy
calculated from the precisely measured beam orbit
position
Quadrature sum of uncertainties due to
beam current measurement                 (10%)
net peak area 0.4 - 28% (mostly <2.0%)
detection efficiency                 (<3%)
gamma lines intensities              (<3%)
Approved by Dr. Lebeda 1 February 2012
